tool.name: SameStr Convert
tool.description: MetaPhlAn or mOTUS marker alignments to nucleotide variant profiles
tool.command:
## The command line tool turns input file names into sample names carried along during ## the entire samestr analysis workflow. ## We either rely on users naming inputs more or less correctly, in which case we still try to prevent ## common problems like using characters that aren't compatible with samestr sample names or ## keeping a redundant file suffix in the input name. ## Alternatively, if the user provided an explicit sample name, we want to name files accordingly. #import re #set $aln_suffix = '.' + $alignment.ext #if str($sample_name): #set $aln_basename = $sample_name #else: #set $aln_basename = re.sub('[^\w\-\.]', '_', $alignment.element_identifier.split($aln_suffix)[0]) #end if ln -s '$alignment' '${aln_basename}${aln_suffix}' && ## tool discovers profile input by identical base name and .profile.txt suffix ln -s '$profile' '${aln_basename}.profile.txt' &&
samestr convert --input-files '${aln_basename}${aln_suffix}' --marker-dir '$database.fields.path' --min-vcov $alignment_param.min_vcov --min-aln-identity $alignment_param.min_aln_identity --min-aln-len $alignment_param.min_aln_len --min-base-qual $alignment_param.min_base_qual --min-aln-qual $alignment_param.min_aln_qual

param.name: sample_name
param.label: Sample name
param.help: If specified, this is used as the name of the sample described by the alignment and profile input above. When left empty, the name of the alignment input dataset (or colection element) will be used.

validator.message: Only alphanumeric characters and any of _, -, . are allowed in sample names.

param.name: database
param.label: SameStr database
param.help: SameStr database built from MetaPhlAn or mOTUS markers

param.label: Minimum alignment quality
param.help: Increasing this threshold can drastically reduce the number of considered variants.

tool.help:
@SAMESTR_INTRO@
SameStr Convert ...............
SameStr Convert processes marker gene alignments from taxonomic profiling tools (MetaPhlAn or mOTUS) to nucleotide variant profiles for strain-level analysis. It outputs per-clade SNV profiles that can be used in downstream SameStr workflow steps.
**Input Requirements**
The following inputs must be provided:
- A BAM dataset containing marker alignments generated by MetaPhlAn or mOTUS. - A taxonomic profile file corresponding to the alignment.
**Alignment Parameters**
- Marker database: A SameStr database generated from a MetaPhlAn or mOTUS clade marker database; the underlying MetaPhlAn or mOTUS database must be the one used to generate the input alignments. - Percent identity: Minimum percent identity between read and marker sequence; alignments with lower identity are filtered out. - Minimum alignment length: Minimum number of aligned bases required; shorter alignments are discarded.
**Results and Outputs**
The tool generates two outputs:
- A table of alignment statistics - A collection of per-clade SNV profiles in compressed NumPy (NPZ) format, i.e. each element of the collection contains the SNV profile for the sample with respect to one clade from the sample's taxonomic profile.
Element identifiers follow the scheme <Clade>.<Sample>, which is the naming scheme expected by SameStr Merge.
